concerns.text: Task T027 mandates 'removing Shapiro-Wilk check' and using Friedman test by default. This violates FR-006 which explicitly requires: 'The system MUST first run a Shapiro-Wilk test... if p < 0.05, it MUST use the Friedman test; otherwise, it MUST use Repeated-Measures ANOVA.' The task silently weakens a mandatory conditional logic requirement into a static default, dropping the ANOVA path entirely.

Task T013 instructs to 'confirm MNAR mechanism ($ho > 0.5$ expected)' but lacks the verification logic to handle failure cases (e.g., flagging or excluding runs). FR-001 and US-1 Acceptance Scenario 1 require the system to 'verify' this correlation. Without explicit logic to handle $ho \le 0.5$, the task allows silent acceptance of invalid simulation data, violating the acceptance criteria's intent to ensure mechanism validity.

Task T027 omits the requirement to 'compute bootstrap confidence intervals for the difference in medians as a robust alternative' if the distribution is skewed. FR-006 explicitly mandates this fallback. The task description only mentions Friedman/Nemenyi, dropping a required robustness check defined in the spec.
